one_line: Founding king of the Imperial Cholas who seized Thanjavur and revived Chola power.

appearance.age_look: Early 40s, athletic and broad-shouldered, with the wiry strength of a seasoned battlefield commander.
appearance.face: Oval face with high cheekbones, medium-brown Dravidian complexion, straight prominent nose, neatly kept thick moustache tapering down the sides, a faint vertical scar on the left eyebrow, default expression calm and commanding with a slight determined frown.
appearance.hair: Long black hair oiled and drawn back into a high warrior’s kudumi (topknot) at the back of the crown, sides smooth and tidy, with a narrow gold circlet-band resting low on the forehead and a small central leaf-shaped boss.
appearance.costume: Sleeveless dark maroon cotton angavastram diagonally across the torso leaving the right shoulder free, wrapped over a fitted off-white cotton upper cloth; waist wrapped in a deep indigo veshti/dhoti reaching mid-calf with a bold gold border, pleated in front and tucked at the navel; a broad gold-plated waist belt with simple engraved floral motifs cinching the veshti; bare feet in simple dark brown leather sandals; jewellery limited to a pair of medium-sized plain gold hoop earrings, a single beaded rudraksha mala around the neck with a small gold pendant, and two plain gold armlets above the biceps; forearms wrapped in dark brown leather bracers; over the left shoulder he sometimes carries a folded dull bronze-hued scale-stitched cloth cuirass (padded armour) that visually sits like a short cape when not worn.
appearance.signature: A long, slightly curved early Chola iron sword with a broad single-edged blade, undecorated steel, a simple straight crossguard, and a dark wooden hilt wrapped in brown leather, carried in a plain dark brown leather scabbard hanging from a diagonal strap across his chest.

ref_desc: Vijayalaya Chola is a medium-brown, oval-faced Tamil king in his early 40s with a neat tapered moustache, high black kudumi topknot, narrow gold forehead band, wearing a dark maroon angavastram over off-white upper cloth, deep indigo gold-bordered veshti, simple gold jewellery, leather bracers and sandals, and carrying a plain curved iron sword in a dark brown leather scabbard, with a palette of maroon, indigo, off-white, gold, and dark brown.
sheet_prompt: Full-body character model of Vijayalaya Chola in anime style: an early-40s, athletic Tamil king with a medium-brown complexion, oval face, straight nose, neat tapered moustache and a faint scar through his left eyebrow; his long black hair is oiled and drawn into a high warrior’s kudumi (topknot) with a narrow gold circlet-band resting low on the forehead, centered by a small leaf-shaped boss. Dress him in a sleeveless dark maroon cotton angavastram draped diagonally across his torso over a fitted off-white upper cloth, leaving the right shoulder free, and a deep indigo veshti/dhoti reaching mid-calf with a bold gold border, neatly pleated and tucked at the navel. At his waist place a broad gold-plated belt with simple engraved floral motifs; add plain gold hoop earrings, a single rudraksha mala with a small gold pendant, two plain gold armlets above the biceps, and dark brown leather bracers on his forearms. Show simple dark brown leather sandals on his feet, and a folded dull bronze-hued padded cloth cuirass slung over his left shoulder like a short cape. His signature weapon is a long slightly curved early Chola iron sword with a broad single-edged undecorated blade, simple straight crossguard, and dark wooden hilt wrapped in brown leather, sheathed in a plain dark brown leather scabbard hanging from a diagonal chest strap. Use a palette dominated by dark maroon, deep indigo, off-white, gold, and dark brown, and present front, side, and back views to clearly show hair, costume, and weapon details.
